description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the `TryEmitLoadForLoadWord64AndShiftRight` function in V8's x64 instruction selector (`src/compiler/backend/x64/instruction-selector-x64.cc`). This function optimizes the pattern of loading a 64-bit value from memory and right-shifting it by 32 bits, by instead directly loading only the upper 32 bits from `original_offset + 4`. The root cause is that the function checks whether the original memory displacement fits within an int32 range (via `ValueFitsIntoImmediate`), but does not verify that `displacement + 4` also fits within int32. When `displacement` is close to `INT32_MAX` (e.g., `0x7FFFFFFC`), adding 4 causes a signed 32-bit integer overflow, wrapping around to a large negative value (e.g., `-2147483648`). This overflowed negative offset is then encoded into the generated machine instruction, causing an out-of-bounds memory read. The vulnerability is triggered by WebAssembly code compiled with TurboFan (--no-liftoff) that performs a 64-bit load from a near-INT32_MAX memory offset followed by a 32-bit unsigned right shift. The JIT instruction selector applies the load optimization, but the offset arithmetic overflows, resulting in a segmentation fault (out-of-bounds read).

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6939347 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
fixes.evidence_text: 89ab7b1efb333b85f5669b10659b8b4abf14bb9b [compiler] Avoid overflow in Load>>32 ISEL optimization
